parsed_review.summary: CP00-264 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (Risk C, 150 units, RP07.P07.M06.S06-RP07.P08.M02.S14) that closes the RP07.P07 failure tail and opens the RP07.P08 Hermes-evidence foundation. The pack-level claim is honestly scoped: production_ready=false, status "implemented_pending_review"/"implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout" — it makes NO premature production or enterprise-trust claim. Source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the claim is supported: (1) SEARCH_CORE_CP264_PACK_BINDING matches the baseline exactly (risk C, 150 units, range, upstream CP00-263, next CP00-265, next_subphase RP07.P08.M02.S15); (2) phase counts P07:106+P08:44=150, micro-phase/micro-title row counts, and the 7-deliverable distribution all reconcile and are computed from real included_units (validators.js:6030 countBy), not hardcoded; (3) all 7 required public exports exist (service.js + validators.js), are surfaced via index.js export *, deep-equaled into contracts/search-core-contract.json, and asserted by both the package test (model.test.js:1876-1927) and the contract validator (validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:1428-1456), with valid===true derived from real error arrays; (4) descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries enforced — freezeCp264Result (service.js:3888) pins ~40 runtime/write flags false and no_real_data/synthetic_only true, and the foundation validator rejects rows that flip descriptor_only/runtime_execution/customer_safe/raw_payload or the cross-tenant/lock/retry/blocked-claim/Hermes/permission/audit/no-real-data/Claude/human guards; (5) authority boundary honored — claude_is_final_approval false, human_final_approval_required true, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval false; (6) Hermes patterns are descriptor/evidence only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false, executes_command_runtime false), not product runtime; (7) review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/source-inspection references, and Claude-final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims. Command-evidence reports all 19 gate commands at exit 0; readiness-report shows auth preflight passed and ready=true. No TODO/FIXME/stub/throw markers in the search src; all upstream references (CP263 builders, searchCoreRowKey, freezeCp264Validation) resolve. Claude remains a read-only reviewer; this verdict is review input, not final approval. One immaterial P3 bookkeeping inconsistency in the review scope artifact; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Not blocking pack closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists new-session-handoff.md in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: The review scope artifact lists docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md simultaneously as an untracked_implementation_files entry (line 96) and as an unrelated_dirty_files_preserved entry (line 102). The 'unrelated' label implies the file is NOT part of the pack, which conflicts with classifying it as a pack implementation file. This is a cosmetic classification ambiguity in a tooling-generated scope artifact only; the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs correctly lists exactly the 8 search-package files, and the unrelated docx/ldip dirty files are correctly excluded. It has no effect on the pack's descriptor-only boundaries, no-write/no-real-data attestations, validation evidence, or the production_ready=false posture.
findings.recommendation: Classify the handoff doc in exactly one bucket (either an in-pack untracked doc or a preserved-but-unrelated dirty file) so the scope artifact is internally consistent. No code or closeout change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: non-blocking; informational only; does not affect pack closeout, boundaries, or the production_ready posture
